blocks.content: He heard the bike before he saw it.

The familiar squeak of Daisy's chain — the one she kept saying she'd oil and never did — coming along the fence line path from Magnolia Street. He'd heard that squeak a hundred times in every direction.
She appeared at the edge of the field and stopped when she saw him.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Oh.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Yeah.
She walked her bike the rest of the way, leaned it against the fence post, and came and stood beside him. Close enough that their arms were almost touching. The fireflies went on with their business.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I come out here most nights in summer.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I know. I've seen you from the porch.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You never came out.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I didn't want to bother you.
She turned to look at him.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You never bother me.
He didn't say anything.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Kit.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I know.
They watched the field for a while.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Do you ever try to catch them?

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: When I was little. Never really could.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I caught one once. Held it in my hands.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Did it glow?

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: No. That's the thing. In your hands they just stop.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: That's kind of sad.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I don't think so. I think they just need the dark for it to work. They're not really for catching.
He thought about that. The field kept blinking around them, indifferent and steady.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Do you think about the future?
She looked at him sideways.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: What kind of question is that.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: A genuine one.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Kit Jenkins asking a genuine question. Mark the calendar.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I'm serious.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I know you are.
She was quiet for a moment, looking out at the field.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Not in a specific way. More like — I just want to still be doing this. Whatever this is.
She gestured at nothing in particular. At all of it. The field, the dark, the town behind them settling into night.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Me too.
A beat.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Don't make it weird.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I wasn't making it weird.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You were about to.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I really wasn't.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Okay.
He hadn't been. Probably. But something about whatever this is had landed somewhere he didn't quite know what to do with — somewhere that felt like more than he had a word for yet.
A firefly came up between them, blinked once, green-gold, and was gone.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Today was really good.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Yeah.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Even the soaked part.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Especially the soaked part.
He looked at her when she said that. She was smiling at the field.
